news.title: Coalition challenges Labor to match $800m fuel reserve pledge
news.snippet: The Coalition has challenged the government to find $800 million in its upcoming budget to match a landmark promise to double the nation's fuel reserves.
news.description: The Coalition has challenged the government to find $800 million in its upcoming budget to match a landmark promise to double the nation's fuel reserves.
news.markdown:
[SKY NEWS LIVE](https://www.skynews.com.au/stream)

**8:02PM** Monday, April 27th, 2026

# Coalition dares Labor to find $800m as Angus Taylor unveils plan to double Australia’s fuel reserves

The Coalition has challenged Labor to match an $800 million fuel security pledge that would double Australia’s mandatory reserves and lift bowser prices by about 1c a litre.

[![Max Aldred](https://resources.news.com.au/author-profiles/d4b16d8e-c9fe-4bde-ab36-19dabe39d265.jpeg)](https://www.skynews.com.au/the-team/max-aldred) [Max Aldred](https://www.skynews.com.au/the-team/max-aldred) Digital Reporter

3 min read

April 28, 2026 - 8:55AM

[Sky News Australia](https://www.skynews.com.au/)[14 Comments](https://www.skynews.com.au/australia-news/politics/coalition-dares-labor-to-find-800m-as-angus-taylor-unveils-plan-to-double-australias-fuel-reserves/news-story/17aaa66af097e3be2d07529d1d17a2ab#comments)

PREMIUM

X

![Next video thumbnail](https://content.api.news/v3/images/bin/e417981a4796b5c4ad9177fc8ebdd13b)

Sky News Australia

Stream Sky News Australia’s live channel which features the country's best journalists, exclusive insights from commentators and ground-breaking special investigations.

moreless

Toggle full description view

The Coalition has challenged the government to find $800 million in its upcoming budget to match a landmark promise to double the nation’s fuel reserves. Watch Opposition Leader Angus Taylor's announcement live.

Angus Taylor and Matt Canavan vowed on Tuesday to lift the nation’s minimum stockholding obligation (MSO) to 60 days – up from 30 days.

The Opposition would do so through a one billion litre, government-funded addition to storage infrastructure.

Forcing suppliers to hold more stocks in reserve would raise per litre prices about 1c at the bowser, the Coalition’s analysis suggested.

It is expected the commitment will be central to Mr Taylor’s budget reply speech, and raises pressure on the Labor government to commit to concrete fuel security measures in the May 12 budget.

Mr Taylor called on the Prime Minister to “pick up” the plan immediately.

![Leader of the Opposition Angus Taylor MP, Leader of The Nationals Matt Canavan MP at the federal parliament. Picture: NewsWire / Martin Ollman.](https://content.api.news/v3/images/bin/9b35574853c1cd25e31338c8fdbd9131)

Leader of the Opposition Angus Taylor MP, Leader of The Nationals Matt Canavan MP at the federal parliament. Picture: NewsWire / Martin Ollman.

Mr Canavan said the taxpayer would foot the $800 million bill for additional onshore storage facilities in “multiple areas and locations”.

However, he threw the details back to private businesses, speaking with Sky News Australia on Tuesday.

“Where that will be is a matter for those businesses that have this obligation imposed on them,” he said.

“They’re in the best position to understand where that is.”

Mr Canavan also revealed the Coalition would look to expand the Fuel Security Services Payment introduced under Scott Morrison in 2021 to subsidise Australia’s two refineries during loss-making periods.

“What we’re saying today is the government should expand that payment to say it would be offered to a new refinery and we’d work out exactly how that might be provided to a new refinery to encourage that,” he said.

“Now a new refinery would obviously help our fuel security situation, and it would also help our storage situation because refineries will clearly have to build storage.”

The Coalition slapped a 2030 deadline on its additional stockholding plan.

Assistant Treasurer Dan Mulino said it was unlikely he and Jim Chalmers would be matching the Coalition's proposal on Tuesday morning.

"I’m not going to be looking to Angus Taylor for policy in this space given his track record and I’m certainly not going to be looking to Angus Taylor for costings on various options,” he told Sky News.

He also defended Labor's response to the fuel crisis, as trade from the Middle East remains choked.

“This government has done a lot of things to increase supply,” Mr Mulino said.

“We’ve seen measures to increase the number of shipments from overseas and the PM himself has travelled to a number of countries.

“We’ve got an agreement with Singapore, we’ve seen the Export Finance Association given powers to help to finance shipments in the world where there’s very volatile prices.

“When it comes to storage, I think (Energy Minister) Chris Bowen has indicated that the government is open to considering a range of other policies… we’ve already released significant reserves and continue to replenish those reserves.”

![The Ampol Lytton refinery. Picture: NewsWire / Sarah Marshall.](https://content.api.news/v3/images/bin/5e508e3ec5d596f6fa38ae1662f20af5)

The Ampol Lytton refinery. Picture: NewsWire / Sarah Marshall.

Mr Bowen and Treasurer Jim Chalmers are understood to be working together on a fuel-security package ahead of the May 12 budget.

It comes as Australia holds 44 days’ petrol, 33 days in diesel and 30 days in jet fuel, according to the energy minister's latest fuel supply update.

Foreign Minister Penny Wong has also travelled to Australia’s Asian allies to shore up ties and fuel supplies.

Ms Wong arrived in Japan on Monday, before heading to Beijing on Wednesday.

It is understood the minister will assure trading partners Australia remains a reliable exporter of gas and importer of fuel.

It comes after the Prime Minister visited Singapore, Malaysia and Brunei earlier in the month to push priority treatment in fuel shipments.

news.markdown:
April 28, 2026

![](https://www.thesaturdaypaper.com.au/sites/all/themes/saturday/images/post_logo_black.svg)

![](https://www.thesaturdaypaper.com.au/sites/default/files/styles/article_large/public/20260331149021854135-original.jpg?itok=1nI5dK4T)

# Taylor proposes $800m fuel plan

SHARE

Copy Link
[Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.thesaturdaypaper.com.au/post/2026/04/28/taylor-proposes-800m-fuel-plan?utm_source=Facebook%26utm_medium=social%26utm_term=read_shared_article%26utm_campaign=social_share_icons%26utm_content=post_share) [X](https://x.com/intent/tweet?text=Taylor%20proposes%20$800m%20fuel%20plan&url=https://www.thesaturdaypaper.com.au/post/2026/04/28/taylor-proposes-800m-fuel-plan?utm_source=Twitter%26utm_medium=social%26utm_term=read_shared_article%26utm_campaign=social_share_icons%26utm_content=post_share%20via%20@SatPaper) [Email](mailto:?subject=Taylor%20proposes%20$800m%20fuel%20plan&body=https://www.thesaturdaypaper.com.au/post/2026/04/28/taylor-proposes-800m-fuel-plan?utm_source=Email%26utm_medium=social%26utm_term=read_shared_article%26utm_campaign=social_share_icons%26utm_content=post_share)

By Kye Halford • April 28, 2026

Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has announced Australia’s fuel stockpile would be doubled under an $800m proposal the Coalition is calling on the federal government to match.

**What we know:**

- **The Coalition promises** Australia would have a 60-day supply of fuel in reserve at all times and at least 1bn litres of extra onshore storage under the policy ( [ABC](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-i/)).

- **Taylor said** a Coalition government would build 1bn litres of new storage in an $800m Australian Fuel Security Facility ( [AFR](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-d/)).

- **The opposition leader** said the Coalition was putting forward a “practical plan” to make sure Australia could “stand on its own two feet” regarding fuel supply. “This is a plan the prime minister should pick up today. No excuses, no delays” ( [ABC](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-h/)).

- **Energy and Climate Change Minister** Chris Bowen revealed on Sunday that Australia had 44 days’ worth of petrol, 33 days of diesel and 30 days of jet fuel in reserve ( [ABC](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-k/)).

- **As energy minister** in the Morrison government, Taylor dismissed a pledge by the then Labor Opposition to expand fuel stockpiles to the International Energy Agency minimum of 90 days, citing the cost ( [AFR](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-u/)).

**Read more:** The Angus Taylor PowerPoint: ‘Political acumen is not his skill’ ( [The Saturday Paper](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-o/))

[Share](mailto:?subject=Taylor%20proposes%20$800m%20fuel%20plan&body=https://www.thesaturdaypaper.com.au/post/2026/04/28/taylor-proposes-800m-fuel-plan#post-1)

# Iran offers to end Strait chokehold

SHARE

Copy Link
[Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.thesaturdaypaper.com.au%2Fpost%2F2026%2F04%2F28%2Firan-offers-end-strait-chokehold?utm_source=Facebook%26utm_medium=social%26utm_term=read_shared_article%26utm_campaign=social_share_icons%26utm_content=post_share) [X](https://x.com/intent/tweet?text=Iran%20offers%20to%20end%20Strait%20chokehold&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.thesaturdaypaper.com.au%2Fpost%2F2026%2F04%2F28%2Firan-offers-end-strait-chokehold?utm_source=Twitter%26utm_medium=social%26utm_term=read_shared_article%26utm_campaign=social_share_icons%26utm_content=post_share%20via%20@SatPaper) [Email](mailto:?subject=Iran%20offers%20to%20end%20Strait%20chokehold&body=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.thesaturdaypaper.com.au%2Fpost%2F2026%2F04%2F28%2Firan-offers-end-strait-chokehold?utm_source=Email%26utm_medium=social%26utm_term=read_shared_article%26utm_campaign=social_share_icons%26utm_content=post_share)

By Kye Halford • April 28, 2026

Iran had offered to end its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for the US lifting its blockade on the country and an end to the war, two regional officials said Monday.

**What we know:**

- **Under the proposal,** discussions on the larger question of its nuclear program would come later – making it an offer US President Donald Trump would be unlikely to accept ( [AP](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-b/)).

- **While in Moscow** to visit Russia’s President Vladimir Putin, Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said America’s leaders wanted to negotiate because they “have achieved none of their goals” in the war ( [AP](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-n/)).

- **During a White House press briefing,** Karoline Leavitt confirmed that Donald Trump had met his national security team on Monday morning to discuss a new Iranian proposal for ending the war ( [The Guardian](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-p/)).

- **Meanwhile, Lebanon and Hezbollah** are at odds over peace talks with Israel, as strikes continue between the two countries despite a ceasefire brokered by the US ( [ABC](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-m/)).

- **At least 14 people were killed** during Israeli strikes on Sunday, with more attacks on Monday. One Israeli soldier was killed and another six injured in a retaliatory drone attack ( [ABC](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-c/)).

**Read more:** Ceasefire holds despite ongoing blockade of strait ( [The Saturday Paper](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-q/))

[Share](mailto:?subject=Iran%20offers%20to%20end%20Strait%20chokehold&body=#post-1)

# RSL Australia to review Welcome to Country policies

SHARE

Copy Link
[Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.thesaturdaypaper.com.au%2Fpost%2F2026%2F04%2F28%2Frsl-australia-review-welcome-country-policies?utm_source=Facebook%26utm_medium=social%26utm_term=read_shared_article%26utm_campaign=social_share_icons%26utm_content=post_share) [X](https://x.com/intent/tweet?text=RSL%20Australia%20to%20review%20Welcome%20to%20Country%20policies&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.thesaturdaypaper.com.au%2Fpost%2F2026%2F04%2F28%2Frsl-australia-review-welcome-country-policies?utm_source=Twitter%26utm_medium=social%26utm_term=read_shared_article%26utm_campaign=social_share_icons%26utm_content=post_share%20via%20@SatPaper) [Email](mailto:?subject=RSL%20Australia%20to%20review%20Welcome%20to%20Country%20policies&body=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.thesaturdaypaper.com.au%2Fpost%2F2026%2F04%2F28%2Frsl-australia-review-welcome-country-policies?utm_source=Email%26utm_medium=social%26utm_term=read_shared_article%26utm_campaign=social_share_icons%26utm_content=post_share)

By Kye Halford • April 28, 2026

RSL Australia will review its Welcome to Country guidelines after Indigenous elders and veterans were booed during Anzac Day services across the country.

**RSL national president Peter Tinley** on Monday said he had been “appalled” by the behaviour at some Anzac Day ceremonies and believed Welcome to Country had a place on Anzac Day, but said the decision to perform the address should fall to individual branches ( [ABC](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-f/)).

**Whadjuk Noongar elder** and army veteran Di Ryder said she had been “mortified” but would not back down after she had been booed while giving a Welcome to Country at Perth’s Anzac Day dawn service ( [ABC](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-z/)).

**Police issued five people** with move-on notices after the heckling, in addition to five that were handed out before the service began ( [ABC](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-v/)).

**It comes as anti-immigration group** Fight for Australia said it had not organised booing at Welcome to Country ceremonies at Anzac Day dawn services, despite a social media post asking followers “how loud will you be this year?” ( [The Guardian](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-e/)).

**Read more:** Stan Gran on the Australia his grandfather fought for ( [The Saturday Paper](https://campaigns.schwartzmedia.com.au/t/i-l-zdhlyo-ittijdkyhi-s/))

[Share](mailto:?subject=RSL%20Australia%20to%20review%20Welcome%20to%20Country%20policies&body=#post-1)

[Kye Halford](https://www.thesaturdaypaper.com.au/contributor/kye-halford)
is a writer and _The Saturday Paper_’s editorial producer.

April 28, 2026

news.title: Taylor proposes $800m plan to double fuel stockpile amid Hormuz closure
news.snippet: Bolstering Australia's fuel stocks has turned into a top-order political issue ahead of the budget and a byelection.
news.description: Bolstering Australia's fuel stocks has turned into a top-order political issue ahead of the budget and a byelection.
news.markdown:
Advertisement

[Add us as a preferred source on Google](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=smh.com.au)

Listen to this article

5 min

Advertisement

Australia’s fuel stockpile would be doubled under an $800 million Coalition proposal that comes as Foreign Minister Penny Wong flies to China, Japan and Korea to scour the region for diesel and petrol deals.

Oil prices rose again after [US-Iran peace talks](https://www.smh.com.au/world/middle-east/iran-reportedly-offers-deal-to-us-to-reopen-strait-and-delay-nuclear-talks-20260427-p5zrfy.html) stalled over the weekend, raising the prospect that the Strait of Hormuz is blocked for even longer as alternative sources of fuel run down.

news.title: Coalition’s $800m plan to solve fuel crisis
news.snippet: The Coalition has unveiled its $800m plan to boost Australia's minimum fuel reserves by more than 50 per cent and provide 1 billion litres of new storage,...
news.description: The Coalition has unveiled its $800m plan to boost Australia's minimum fuel reserves by more than 50 per cent and provide 1 billion litres of new storage,...
news.markdown:
# Coalition puts forward an $800m plan to double fuel reserves and provide more storage

The Coalition has unveiled its $800m plan to secure fuel reserves and provide an additional 1bn litres of storage, amid ongoing fallout from the Middle East conflict.

Emma Kirk

2 min read

April 27, 2026 - 10:12PM

NewsWire

![](https://content.api.news/v3/images/bin/2ccaa405be1c470f39cf9b7eb08902cd)

An extra 200 million litres of diesel are headed for Australian shores, the Prime Minister has announced, with the supplies secured through the federal government’s export finance scheme. Two cargoes are coming from South Korea, one from Brunei and another from Malaysia, Anthony Albanese told reporters in Sydney. Energy Minister Chris Bowen said the extra cargoes would provide “extra supply to give us an extra buffer” and would arrive in Australia by the last week of May or the first week of June.

The Coalition has unveiled its $800m plan to boost Australia’s minimum fuel reserves by more than 50 per cent and provide 1 billion litres of new storage, amid global fallout from the ongoing Middle East conflict.

The Opposition has called on the Government to introduce a new minimum stock level that will increase critical fuel stockholding by almost 25 per cent, with a focus on diesel.

Opposition leader Angus Taylor said it was a plan that should be picked up by the Prime Minister without delay.

![Opposition leader Angus Taylor has called on the Government to adopt a new $800m plan put forward by the Coalition to boost the nation’s minimum fuel reserves and provide additional storage. Picture: NewsWire / Martin Ollman.](https://content.api.news/v3/images/bin/d5767c0f5573e2f50cdc91eae025b7a1)

Opposition leader Angus Taylor has called on the Government to adopt a new $800m plan put forward by the Coalition to boost the nation’s minimum fuel reserves and provide additional storage. Picture: NewsWire / Martin Ollman.

“If fuel stops, Australia stops. It’s that simple. Trucks don’t move, supermarkets don’t stock, businesses shut their doors,” he said.

“We are putting forward a practical plan to make sure that never happens. More fuel in reserve, more storage on the ground, and a country that can stand on its own two feet.

“This is about protecting Australians’ way of life and restoring their standard of living. You don’t do that with talk. You do it with action.”

The Coalition’s plan involves more than doubling fuel reserves by increasing the Minimum Stockholding Obligation (MSO) to 60 days.

It will also establish an $800m Australian Fuel Security Facility to deliver at least 1 billion litres of new onshore storage - with a focus on diesel.

The Nationals Leader Matt Canavan said the plan would keep more fuel in Australia with less reliance on overseas supplies. Picture: NewsWire / Martin Ollman.

The Nationals Leader Matt Canavan said families and businesses needed confidence that fuel would be there when they needed it.

“People in the regions know how serious this is. If the diesel doesn’t turn up, the farm doesn’t run and the shelves go empty,” he said.

“This plan is just common sense. Keep more fuel here in Australia so we are not relying on overseas supply lines that can be cut overnight.”

“We cannot keep hoping for the best. We need to be ready, and this plan gets us there.”

Under the existing MSO, fuel suppliers are required to hold a minimum stock of fuel with costs passed through to consumers in the final price of fuel.

Fuel suppliers would need to construct additional storage and purchase additional fuel - achieved with added financial support from the government.

It is understood the Coalition estimates that the doubling the existing MSO would raise the price of fuel at the bowser by around 1c per litre.

Shadow Minister for Energy and Emissions Reduction Dan Tehan said the Coalition’s plan delivered a clear pathway to stronger fuel security.

“This is a practical, achievable plan that strengthens our fuel supply chain and backs Australian industry,” Mr Tehan said.

“It works with industry, builds storage where it is needed, and makes sure we have the buffer to withstand global shocks.”

The party claims they could deliver 60 days of fuel security by 2030 if the proposal starts today.
